text: Time is a unique and finite resource. And so, it is wasted all around us. Wasted in so many areas: environment, economy and society. But not least, it is wasted for other people and living beings – as well as nature itself. Consequently, the wasting of our time, but also the time of others, becomes a decisive obstacle to preventing crises like climate change or to preventing violent conflicts in various parts of the world. We no longer look at the wasting of our time and that of others in this context – yet our way of dealing with time and time structures, which we call temporament, also plays a decisive role in conflict prevention and the preservation of lasting peace. In order to fundamentally secure the future of humanity, society and nature, a new temporament and corresponding time forms must be developed from scratch in the context of peacework. To this end, we first of all need to establish the time conception and the necessary characteristics of the temporal conditions that we encounter in everyday life and in our professional environment and that are – without exception – incompatible with the desired time forms for peacework.
explanation: The text employs elevated, abstract rhetoric and some coined terminology, with a level of polish typical of AI-generated or translated content. However, it could also be a human academic piece, so the signal is suggestive but not conclusive.
